findings.title: Integration with Stripe for Invoicing
findings.description: The implementation of the usage-based billing engine (CM-301) is dependent on integrating with Stripe for invoicing. This integration is critical for processing payments based on consumption metrics.
assumptions: Stripe API is stable and well-documented.

evidence.excerpt: Requires integration with Stripe for invoicing.

recommendations: Initiate discussions with Stripe for integration requirements.

findings.title: Customer Churn Risk During Pricing Migration
findings.description: There is a significant risk of customer churn during the transition to usage-based pricing, especially for mid-market accounts. A churn risk analysis model (CM-305) is needed to identify at-risk customers.
assumptions: Customer segments have been accurately defined.

evidence.excerpt: Flag accounts where projected spend increases >30% for proactive account management outreach.

recommendations: Develop and implement the churn risk analysis model as a priority.

findings.title: Phased Delivery Plan for Pricing Migration
findings.description: Propose a phased delivery plan where the MVP includes the billing engine (CM-301) and pricing calculator (CM-302), V1 includes spending alerts (CM-303) and grandfathering system (CM-304), and V2 focuses on churn risk analysis (CM-305).
assumptions: All components can be developed independently.

evidence.excerpt: Design and implement usage-based billing engine.
Build pricing calculator and cost estimator for customers.

recommendations: Follow the proposed phased delivery plan to manage risk and ensure customer satisfaction.

findings.title: Real-time Metering Pipeline Requirement
findings.description: The usage-based billing engine (CM-301) requires a real-time metering pipeline to track usage. This is a foundational component that must be built before the billing engine can function.
assumptions: Existing infrastructure can support real-time data processing.

evidence.excerpt: Requires a metering pipeline that tracks usage in real time.

recommendations: Assess current infrastructure capabilities for real-time processing.

findings.title: Compliance Risk with Grandfathering Customers
findings.description: Implementing a grandfathering system (CM-304) may pose compliance risks if not handled correctly, especially regarding customer notifications and pricing changes.
assumptions: Legal requirements for customer notifications are clear.

evidence.excerpt: Need a grandfathering system that tracks customer cohorts.

recommendations: Consult with legal to ensure compliance with grandfathering practices.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies on Stripe integration and real-time metering for the billing engine, along with risks related to customer churn and compliance during the pricing migration. A phased delivery plan is recommended to mitigate these risks and ensure a smooth transition to usage-based pricing.
